verdict: Tiny $13 ad spend produced 24 purchases worth $4,500 — proof of concept, but volume is the missing piece.
diagnosis: The Muslim Subscription Box campaign is converting efficiently on a paper-thin budget; underfunding, not creative, is now the ceiling on growth.

signals.headline: Only $13.21 spent in 28 days
signals.detail: Paid budget is too small to learn or scale. Meta needs roughly 50 conversions weekly to optimize properly.

signals.headline: 24 purchases generated $4,500 revenue
signals.detail: Average order value near $187 is strong for DTC meat. The subscription box offer is clearly resonating with the audience.

signals.headline: 66 add-to-carts, only 24 purchases
signals.detail: Roughly one in three cart adds becomes a sale. Checkout friction or shipping cost is leaking revenue.

signals.headline: 952 new follows, 6,628 total
signals.detail: Audience growth is healthy this period. Organic interest is building faster than paid spend can capture it.

signals.headline: 270 reviews logged this period
signals.detail: Strong local trust signal on Google. Worth surfacing on product pages and ad creative to lift conversion.

benchmark.label: Benchmark
benchmark.subheader: Food DTC cost per sale runs $45–$53
benchmark.body: Food and beverage has the lowest customer acquisition cost in DTC at $45–$53. Instagram cost per purchase for ecommerce typically lands between $15 and $60 depending on order value and funnel.

seasonal.label: Seasonal
seasonal.subheader: Q1–Q2 is the cheapest ad window
seasonal.body: Instagram cost per thousand impressions hit a January 2026 low of $15.74 before Q4 spikes 30–50%. Summer is the best value window to test creative and build audiences before fall.

competitor.label: Category
competitor.subheader: Subscription meat boxes face rising costs
competitor.body: 88% of subscription brands report higher acquisition costs in 2025. Meal kit churn runs 12–18% monthly, so winning brands lean on email and SMS, which drive 30–35% of revenue.

recommendations.title: Scale the Muslim Subscription Box budget meaningfully
recommendations.detail: Consider raising daily spend to at least $30–$50 so Meta can exit the learning phase. Current $0.50/day spend is too thin to gather reliable signal.

recommendations.title: Fix the cart-to-purchase drop with checkout testing
recommendations.detail: Two-thirds of add-to-carts never convert. Test free shipping thresholds, express checkout, and an exit-intent offer. Each point of recovery is direct revenue.

recommendations.title: Test Reels placement and short-form video creative
recommendations.detail: Reels run roughly 20–40% cheaper than Feed this year. Worth shifting budget toward vertical video featuring product close-ups and customer reviews.
